payload.text:
# Google AI Studio Prompt — UCEMS UI Mockup (Copy & Paste)

You are a professional UI/UX designer. Create a clean, modern, web-based UI mockup for a system called **University Club Event Management System (UCEMS)** at Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT).

## System Goal

UCEMS is a centralized platform that replaces manual event proposal submissions (printed documents, email, WhatsApp, etc.) with an organized online workflow that improves tracking, approval, and document storage.

## Core Users / Roles (Role-based access control)

Design screens for these roles:

1. **Club Representative** (submits proposals, checks status, uploads post-event reports)
2. **Club Advisor** (reviews and approves proposals, gives feedback / requests changes)
3. **MPP Club EXCO** (final proposal approval, can request amendments)
4. **HEPA Staff** (monitor proposals/events, ensure compliance, can leave regulatory advice)

---

# UI Pages to Generate (Must Include)

## 1) Landing Page

* UCEMS branding (UMT)
* Sign in / Register buttons
* Brief description of system

## 2) Registration Page

Form fields:

* Full Name
* Matric ID / Staff ID
* Email
* Password + Confirm Password
* Role selection dropdown (Club Rep, Advisor, MPP EXCO, HEPA)
* Validation states (error + success)

## 3) Login Page

* Email
* Password
* Forgot password link
* Login button
* Role detected automatically after login

## 4) Role-Based Dashboards

### Club Representative Dashboard

* Quick stats cards: Total Proposals, Pending, Needs Improvement, Approved
* “Submit New Proposal” CTA button
* Table/list of proposals with filters (Status, Date)
* Proposal tracking timeline widget

### Club Advisor Dashboard

* Tabs: Pending Reviews, Reviewed, Approved
* Proposal queue list
* Quick review actions

### MPP Club EXCO Dashboard

* Final approval queue
* Filter by club, status, date
* Proposal review and decision panel

### HEPA Staff Dashboard

* Monitoring dashboard
* View all proposals + statuses
* Compliance notes section
* Export/report option (basic button only)

---

## 5) Submit Event Proposal Page (Club Rep)

Form fields:

* Event Title
* Club Name
* Event Date & Time
* Venue (text only, no booking feature)
* Objective
* Target Participants
* Description / Tentative schedule
* Risk Assessment upload
* Proposal document upload
* Submit button
  After submission: show status = **Pending Approval**

## 6) Proposal Details Page (Shared Screen)

Include:

* Proposal summary panel (title, club, date, status)
* Uploaded documents section (download/view links)
* Approval timeline (Advisor → MPP EXCO → Completed)
* Activity log/history panel

## 7) Feedback & Revision Screen

If status = **Needs Improvement**:

* Display feedback comments from Advisor/MPP
* Highlight required sections
* “Edit Proposal” button
* “Resubmit” button
* Show version history (v1, v2, v3)

## 8) Review Proposal Screen (Advisor + MPP EXCO)

Layout:

* Proposal preview panel
* Documents panel
* Comment box
  Actions (with confirmation modal):
* Approve
* Request Amendments (sets status = Needs Improvement)
* Reject (sets status = Rejected)

## 9) Post-Event Report Upload Screen (Club Rep)

Visible only when proposal is **Approved**:

* Upload report file (PDF/doc)
* Upload photos (optional)
* Submit report button
* Confirmation message
* Event archived after submission

---

# Navigation (Sidebar)

* Dashboard
* Proposals

  * Submit Proposal (Club Rep)
  * My Proposals (Club Rep)
  * Review Queue (Advisor/MPP)
* Events (Approved)
* Post-Event Reports
* Notifications
* Profile / Settings
* Logout

---

# Design Style Requirements

* Modern university admin system UI
* Consistent components: cards, tables, sidebar navigation
* Colors: professional (UMT-friendly navy/blue + white background)
* Minimal, user-friendly, high usability
* Clear labels & progress indicators
* Status icons for: Pending, Approved, Needs Improvement, Rejected
* Responsive layout (desktop-first, adaptable to mobile)

---

# Output Format

Generate:

1. A UI mockup set (all screens above)
2. A navigation menu hierarchy (sidebar)
3. Short notes on UI flow for each role

Focus on clarity, efficiency, and structured approval workflow.

**Planning the Architecture**

I'm now focusing on the architecture for the UCEMS React application.  The multi-role structure is key, so I'm thinking through how to best handle permissions and access control. Considering how to map the roles to specific UI components is underway, and I am also beginning to flesh out the data models. The basic page structure will be: Landing, Auth, then distinct role-based dashboards.


**Mapping Out the Pages**

My focus is now on the specific pages and features. I've broken down the necessary pages: landing, login, register, role-based dashboards, and crucial screens for proposal submission, review, feedback, and post-event reporting. I'm also planning the data persistence approach, leaning towards `localStorage` or in-memory mock data to simulate state. The goal is to centrally manage this state. UI design is top of mind as well; clean tables, cards, sidebars, and UMT branding are the objectives.


**Building the UI Structure**

I'm deep into component development, focusing on reusable UI elements within the `components/` directory. Currently, I'm working on the `Layout` component, aiming for a sidebar that dynamically adjusts based on the user's role. I've also created the `StatusBadge` for consistent display of proposal statuses.  Furthermore, I am planning a centralized mock data structure for proposals, events, and reports, including a basic user simulation for authentication testing. The next step is fleshing out the public-facing pages: Landing, Login, and Register.


**Defining Key Flows**

I've been mapping out the specific flows and key screens. For a club rep, the focus is the submit proposal form and post-event reporting. Everyone will access proposal details, and advisors/MPPs will have a dedicated review screen. Now, I'm integrating the Tailwind colors—primarily navy blue. `Lucide-react` will be the standard icon library. I also have updated the file list structure. React router will be used.


**Outlining Page Components**

I'm now focusing on the fundamental page structures. I've got my initial page component list: `Landing`, `Auth` (with login/register toggle), `Dashboard`, `SubmitProposal`, `ProposalDetails`, `ReviewProposal`, and `PostEventReport`.  I will keep the Login and Register pages distinct but easy to switch. I'll integrate these components into the `App.tsx` file for routing. Now, it's time to build!

Main application layout with Sidebar and Header
Public landing page

payload.text: finish the profile management page

**Constructing the Profile Page**

I've initiated the Profile page creation. The `/profile` route now necessitates a dedicated component. I'm building out `pages/Profile.tsx` to replace that placeholder.


**Building Profile Features**

I'm now focusing on the profile page's functionalities. I've designed a standard university profile with sections for viewing and editing details. The profile will display core data like name and email. I've also incorporated mock editing capabilities for the name and password, and will display relevant club information. Notifications and appearance preferences will be added as toggles. I'm currently setting up the `Profile.tsx` page to integrate this design with the existing authentication context.


**Refining User Update Logic**

I've just added an `updateUser` function to the `AuthContext` to manage immediate UI updates. I've successfully modified the `AuthContext.tsx` file, incorporating `updateUser`.  The implementation updates the user state based on provided partial data, maintaining consistency across the app. This will allow the profile page to reflect any changes as soon as they are made.


**Incorporating Notifications & App**

I'm now integrating notification settings into the profile, with checkboxes for email and browser alerts. I've also updated `App.tsx` to include the profile page. I am updating my previous design to include a read-only ID field. Refactoring is required and I am taking steps to implement the modifications.


**Enhancing Profile Forms**

I'm now incorporating form components into the profile page. I've designed the profile with state management for editable fields like name and password. I'm also adding notification preferences with email and browser options. Form validation and error/success messaging will be essential features of this update.


**Adding UI for Account Settings**

I've just designed a functional account settings section within the profile. This includes name and password change forms, complete with basic validation and feedback messages for success or errors. I've built in error handling for incorrect password entries and mismatched/weak new passwords. The implementation simulates API calls with delays and mock responses. I'm aiming for a seamless user experience.


**Developing Profile UI Elements**

I'm presently building the User Interface (UI) components for the profile. I'm focusing on incorporating visual elements for success and error messages, employing Tailwind CSS classes for dynamic styling. I've designed the profile card to reflect the user's name, email, and role, including a visually distinctive profile picture. This component is now being integrated into the main profile structure.


**Refining Preferences Settings**

I'm currently working on enhancing the UI for notification preferences. I'm adding toggles for email and browser alerts within the profile preferences section. I've integrated visual cues with Tailwind classes for a sleek, interactive design. I'm setting state to manage these toggle values, ensuring the UI accurately reflects user choices.


**Adding Interactive Preferences**

I'm now implementing the browser notification toggle with visual feedback. I'm adding a switch-like design with Tailwind CSS for an engaging user experience. The toggle will update its appearance based on the `browserNotif` state, which is managed reactively. I am ensuring that the interactive elements are neatly integrated into the preferences section. I'm also adding a conditional display for the edit button to indicate editing availability.


**Designing the Name Field**

I'm currently working on the name field within the profile settings.  I've incorporated a user icon and added visual styling. I've also incorporated the editing state to enable and disable it appropriately.  I'm making sure it aligns with the overall design, with consideration for responsiveness.


**Adding Email and Role Display**

I've just added the email and role fields to the profile section. I've used input elements, set to be disabled, showing the user's email and role. I've incorporated icons for visual clarity, using Tailwind CSS classes for a clean and disabled appearance. The email input will display the email address and is disabled. The role input similarly displays a user's role and is disabled.


**Adding Club Display**

I've just incorporated the club name field into the profile, displaying it conditionally. The existing UI now shows the club name when available.  I've disabled the input field and styled it to match other read-only inputs. I'm leveraging the `user.clubName` property to control the display.


**Integrating Security Settings**

I'm now integrating the password change form into the profile. I've built the structure and styled it, including a cancel and save button, and error handling for common mistakes. I am working to ensure that the form submits the password changes via API, with suitable handling for different responses. I'm focusing on UI/UX with smooth feedback to the user.


**Adding Final Form Elements**

I'm wrapping up the form elements in the profile. I've integrated the current and new password fields, adding styling with Tailwind and input validation. The `currentPassword`, `newPassword`, and `confirmPassword` states manage the input values. I'm focusing on error handling and clean visual feedback.


**Concluding Profile Implementation**

I've finalized the profile page with the integrated password change form. I've ensured seamless functionality, focusing on user experience. All form elements are now in place, from name updates to password changes. Error handling is complete and visually informative, and the design now provides a clear and interactive profile section, which looks very polished. I've finished the form, and the profile is now functional.


**Enhancing the Context**

The `AuthContext.tsx` is updated to include an `updateUser` function within its interface and implementation. I've added `updateUser` to the `AuthContextType` interface and incorporated it within the `AuthProvider` component. I'm modifying the implementation to allow the `updateUser` to directly modify the `user` state, enabling the profile page to update immediately.

To complete the profile management page, I will create a new `Profile` component that allows users to view their details, edit their name, update their password (simulated), and toggle notification preferences. I will also update the `AuthContext` to support updating user data in the app state.

Here are the changes:

1.  **Update `contexts/AuthContext.tsx`**: Add an `updateUser` function to allow the profile page to modify the current user's state.
2.  **Create `pages/Profile.tsx`**: Implement the UI for the profile settings.
3.  **Update `App.tsx`**: Wire up the new `/profile` route to the `Profile` component.

payload.text: create the Edit & Resubmit page for the club rep

**Reviewing the Proposal Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on the flow for the "Edit & Resubmit" process. Examining `ProposalDetails.tsx` reveals that the "Edit & Resubmit" button is already in place. This simplifies matters, as much of the groundwork is laid. Now, I'll assess what changes are needed to make it functional.


**Refining Edit Logic**

I'm presently dissecting the `EditProposal` page component. I plan to pre-populate the form with existing proposal details, echoing the structure of the `SubmitProposal` component. My focus now is on implementing the update operation, ensuring a status change back to `PENDING_ADVISOR` upon resubmission. I'm also updating `DataContext` to allow updating various proposal fields beyond just status.


**Constructing Edit Route**

I've scoped out the editing process. I'm focusing on `/proposals/:id/edit` as the route. I'll hook up `ProposalDetails.tsx` to use this new route for the "Edit & Resubmit" button. I'm getting into the meat of `EditProposal.tsx`, addressing data fetching, authorization, and form initialization. I've begun to define the `DataContext` alterations, aiming for a flexible `updateProposal` method that manages both data updates and the `PENDING_ADVISOR` status upon resubmission.


**Adding the Edit Function**

I'm presently integrating an `editProposal` function into `DataContext.tsx`. It will accept an ID and a partial proposal, merge the data, set the status to `PENDING_ADVISOR`, and append a history entry indicating resubmission by the submitter. This will ensure that our edit and resubmit workflow maintains auditability. The next step involves creating the `EditProposal.tsx` page to handle the UI interaction.


**Codifying Edit Flow**

I'm now implementing the `EditProposal.tsx` page. I've mostly duplicated `SubmitProposal.tsx` and am integrating `useParams` for the proposal ID and `useEffect` to populate the form. I'm handling the "Cancel" functionality to return to the details. The "Submit" button will initiate the `editProposal` function I designed. I've added a new route in `App.tsx` and updated the button in `ProposalDetails.tsx` to link to the edit route. I'll maintain the modern UI and the title "Edit & Resubmit Proposal". A check for a missing proposal will redirect to the dashboard.

To implement the "Edit & Resubmit" functionality, I will:

1.  **Update `contexts/DataContext.tsx`**: Add an `editProposal` function to handle updating proposal details and resetting the status to `PENDING_ADVISOR`.
2.  **Create `pages/EditProposal.tsx`**: A new page allowing Club Representatives to edit existing proposal details (Title, Venue, Date, etc.) and resubmit them.
3.  **Update `App.tsx`**: Add the route `/proposals/:id/edit`.
4.  **Update `pages/ProposalDetails.tsx`**: Wire up the "Edit & Resubmit" button to navigate to the new page.

entries.description: Add editProposal function to handle proposal resubmission
New page to edit and resubmit proposals
Add route for Edit Proposal
Link the Edit & Resubmit button to the new edit page

payload.text: finish off the approved events page on all reviewer page and hepa staff so the approved event appear

**Focusing Event Creation**

I'm currently focused on the "Approved Events" page. The initial route is in place, but I need to flesh out the actual calendar functionality. I'm thinking about how to integrate a calendar component that can display the approved events dynamically. Right now, it's just a placeholder, but the goal is to make it interactive and user-friendly. I will be looking into using a calendar library soon.


**Building Approved Events Page**

I've made progress on the Approved Events page. I've built the new `ApprovedEvents.tsx` component and integrated it into `App.tsx`, replacing the placeholder. I'm pulling data from the `useData` context, filtering the `proposals` based on their status (APPROVED or COMPLETED). I'm leaning toward a table or list-style display, consistent with the dashboard's design, to present event details clearly.


**Developing Event Filters**

I'm now integrating a filter input for searching events within the "Approved Events" page. I'm considering specific data needs for HEPA staff and ensuring approved events are displayed. The plan involves updating the `App.tsx` file to properly import and use the new `ApprovedEvents` component, which I'll refine further.



**Adding Search Functionality**

I've implemented a search filter within `ApprovedEvents.tsx`. Now, I'm working on the user experience around it, ensuring it's responsive and easy to use. I am filtering the events by title and club name. I'm also planning to integrate this search alongside the existing data fetching from the `useData` context.


**Enhancing Event Display**

I'm currently working to improve the presentation of approved events. I've integrated a new table to the Approved Events page with the following columns: Date, Event Title, Club Name, Location, Description, and Action. This table will use data from the `useData` context, filtering proposals with an 'APPROVED' or 'COMPLETED' status. I'm exploring adding an action column for future interactive features. I'm making it visually consistent with the rest of the dashboard.


**Improving Event Details Table**

I'm now revising the table in the "Approved Events" section. I've added a "Status" column to indicate whether an event is approved or completed. I'm focusing on ensuring data consistency and visual coherence. The goal is to make the information clear and easy to find, so users can understand the event details at a glance. I'm also planning to incorporate more descriptive information about each event to improve usability.


**Refining Event Actions**

I'm now revising the event action column. I've switched the "View" button to be a clickable link that dynamically navigates users to event details. I've also incorporated the navigation using the `useNavigate` hook, ensuring a smooth transition. I'm focusing on user experience, aiming for seamless and intuitive event interaction. This is the last feature to add to the table.


**Updating Page Navigation**

I've integrated the Approved Events page with the application's main navigation. I've ensured this page is now accessible to all logged-in users. This was accomplished by adding the necessary imports and route within `App.tsx`. I'm confident the page will display correctly once the application reloads with the current setup. I've also incorporated the correct icon and label.

To finish the "Approved Events" page, I will create a new component `pages/ApprovedEvents.tsx` that lists all proposals with `APPROVED` or `COMPLETED` status. This page will be accessible to all roles (Club Reps, Advisors, MPP, HEPA Staff) via the sidebar.

Here are the changes:

1.  **Create `pages/ApprovedEvents.tsx`**: A clean, searchable table view of all approved events.
2.  **Update `App.tsx`**: Replace the placeholder route with the new component.

entries.description: New page to display list of approved and completed events

import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { useData } from '../contexts/DataContext'; import { ProposalStatus } from '../types'; import { StatusBadge } from '../components/StatusBadge'; import { Calendar, MapPin, Search } from 'lucide-react'; import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; const ApprovedEvents: React.FC = () => { const { proposals } = useData(); const navigate = useNavigate(); const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState(''); // Filter for Approved or Completed events // Sort by event date (ascending - upcoming first) const approvedEvents = proposals .filter(p => (p.status === ProposalStatus.APPROVED || p.status === ProposalStatus.COMPLETED) && (p.title.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase()) || p.clubName.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase()) || p.venue.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase())) ) .sort((a, b) => new Date(a.eventDate).getTime() - new Date(b.eventDate).getTime()); return (

Approved Events

Official calendar of upcoming and completed university club events.

setSearchTerm(e.target.value)} />

payload.text: HEPA staff may enter monitoring notes or compliance feedback for a proposal/event. Inputs include comment fields and optional tagging or referencing for record purposes.

**Defining User Permissions**

I'm currently focused on defining the specific user permissions needed.  I've nailed down that HEPA staff need to be able to enter notes or compliance feedback. I'm exploring the most efficient way to check the UserRole, likely using a simple conditional check for "HEPA_STAFF". Next step is to design the interface for inputting these notes.


**Adding Compliance Notes**

I've just added a new `compliance_note` type to the `Comment` interface within `types.ts`, which will allow HEPA staff to input their feedback directly. The plan now is to modify `ProposalDetails.tsx` to include an input form for these notes.  I envision this residing near existing comments, with a clear distinction to prevent confusion. The underlying data structure will neatly incorporate compliance feedback. This approach leverages the established comment system for efficiency.


**Integrating Compliance Input**

I'm now integrating the compliance note input into the `ProposalDetails` page. I've confirmed that the existing `addComment` function in `DataContext.tsx` should work with a modified `Comment` type. My plan is to include a role-based check within `ProposalDetails.tsx` to display an input form specifically for HEPA staff. I'll need to update `types.ts` to accommodate the new note type before proceeding.


**Developing Tagging Functionality**

I'm now integrating the tagging input feature for compliance notes. Alongside the text area and submit button, I've included a simple text input field for reference or tagging purposes. Upon submission, the modified `addComment` function will handle the new "hepaTag" input. I intend to visually differentiate these notes in the comments display by using a distinct background or icon. I also need to update the data context to ensure proper handling of the tagging information. I'm focusing on ensuring the user experience is intuitive and efficient.


**Confirming Type Integration**

I've confirmed that the type update, specifically the addition of `referenceTag`, integrates smoothly within `types.ts`.  The `DataContext.tsx` update ensures the `addComment` function passes through the new `referenceTag` property without issue.  I am now focusing on the interface design within `ProposalDetails.tsx` to display the "hepaTag" input field conditionally based on user role and the successful handling of different comment types. I'm prioritizing an intuitive design for compliance note entry.


**Refining Note Display**

I'm currently working to integrate the "hepaTag" within the proposal details display. I'm modifying `ProposalDetails.tsx` to include the display of compliance notes submitted by HEPA staff. I'll need to clearly distinguish these notes visually from other comments using a new css class. I plan to use the author role to determine display for each comment. The type definition now includes the tag which will be displayed. This should provide a clear and organized view.


**Handling Tagged Compliance Notes**

I've just added the "tag" property to the `Comment` type and integrated it with the `addComment` function in `DataContext`. This approach simplifies updates to `types.ts` without needing to change `DataContext.tsx`. I've added a section with conditional rendering to the display for the "hepaTag". I'm now testing the overall flow.


**Implementing Role Checks**

I've just added checks to ensure the `DataContext` imports the updated `Comment` type without any errors. Then, I updated `types.ts` to include a new field: `tag` in `Comment` interface. Next, I've started the integration of the input feature in `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I've added state variables, `hepaNote` and `hepaTag`, and the `handleHepaSubmit` handler, ensuring comments are linked to user and role and tagging is saved with notes. The goal is to make a smooth input, but only for the HEPA user.


**Incorporating New Input**

I realize that to add the compliance note input in `ProposalDetails.tsx`, I'll need to make the first explicit usage of `addComment` within that component. While existing code primarily uses `updateProposalStatus`, this new feature necessitates calling `addComment` directly.  I'll need to carefully structure the input form and ensure that the correct `type` and `tag` values are passed.  I need to integrate `hepaTag` and the text. This will involve the state management of the `hepaTag` which will involve an additional type.


**Updating Note Input**

I'm now focusing on integrating the new input fields within `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I've started on the interface elements, including a dedicated section for "HEPA Compliance & Monitoring." This will encompass a textarea for the note and an optional text input for a tag/reference. My aim is to make the experience smooth. I'm also ensuring the correct `Comment` type gets passed and will check the overall display.


**Designing Tag Input**

I'm now designing the tag input for compliance notes. I've updated `types.ts` to include the `tag` and `compliance_note` type. The plan now is to integrate this into `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I think I'll add an input field next to the note area. I'm focusing on user interface design. I also considered data flow. Now the challenge is adding the input without breaking the general input flow.


**Implementing Note Styling**

I'm now implementing the visual styling for compliance notes, ensuring they are clearly distinguishable from other comment types. I've successfully added a conditional CSS class (`bg-indigo-50` with an indigo border) to the `Comment` interface based on the `compliance_note` type.  I've also integrated the display of the `tag` within the comment itself.  My plan is to put the input at the bottom. The next step is to get the inputs and submit button working.


**Confirming Type Update**

I'm checking that the `tag` property can be successfully added to the `Comment` interface within `types.ts` without breaking existing functionality.  I'm now going to add a new `compliance_note` type to the comment type, as well as the new `tag` property, ensuring the data structure remains consistent. This will enable me to seamlessly incorporate the tagging feature into the proposal comments.


**Integrating Submission Handling**

I'm now focusing on integrating the submission handling within `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I've added state variables, `hepaNote` and `hepaTag`, and the `handleHepaSubmit` handler, ensuring comments are linked to user and role and tagging is saved with notes. I'm aiming for a smooth input, accessible only to HEPA users, and the submission flow needs to incorporate tagging. The next goal is error handling and styling.


**Finalizing Compliance Input**

I'm working to add the compliance note input specifically for HEPA users within `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I've added state variables `hepaNote` and `hepaTag`, and the `handleHepaSubmit` handler, ensuring comments are linked to user and role and tagging is saved with notes. I've designed the input fields and ensured the `addComment` function is correctly invoked with the new data. My current focus is to ensure the interface is clean and the user experience is optimal.


**Handling Amendment Comments**

I'm now focusing on integrating the existing handling of amendments and rejections, which require comments, with the new compliance note feature.  I've reviewed the existing `handleAction` function and ensured that it still requires comments for amendments and rejections. I've now integrated the new `handleHepaSubmit` function to work with `handleAction`.  The user experience should be smooth and intuitive.


**Designing UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on integrating the existing handling of amendments and rejections, which require comments, with the new compliance note feature. I'm working on the design of the action modal with new styling. I need to get the inputs and submit button working.


**Implementing Action Handling**

The existing system's amend/reject comments are now integrated with compliance notes. I've ensured `handleAction` still requires comments for amendments/rejections. The key addition is the integration with my new `handleHepaSubmit`. The design has been updated and the user can now create comments while amending.



**Streamlining Compliance Workflow**

I'm currently working to integrate the submission handling within `ProposalDetails.tsx`, ensuring HEPA staff can smoothly input compliance notes alongside existing functionality. I've integrated `hepaNote`, `hepaTag`, and the `handleHepaSubmit` handler, making sure comments are linked correctly. The goal is to create a seamless and efficient workflow.


**Integrating Action Buttons**

I'm now integrating the proposal actions into the application. I've designed the action buttons for Approve, Request Changes, and Reject, making sure they function correctly with the existing system and seamlessly incorporate the new compliance note feature. The UI is now much more intuitive. I need to make sure the state is handled correctly.


**Designing Comment Integration**

I've just finished integrating the compliance note input directly within the existing comment flow. I've focused on ensuring a consistent user experience by visually integrating it within the comments section. The submission logic, including role checks and tagging, is now fully functional, as are the existing action buttons. The entire flow is working now.


**Finalizing Comment Workflow**

I'm putting the finishing touches on the comment section within the proposal details view. I've ensured a smooth and intuitive display of compliance notes alongside regular comments, with correct handling of user roles, tagging, and submission. All aspects of the comment section are now functioning.


**Refining Note Workflow**

1) Registration Output

Output Design:
After submission, the system displays a success message confirming account creation or error messages if input validation fails. Users are redirected to the login page upon successful registration. do not automatically login user when done registring

**Updating Registration Flow**

I'm currently focused on the registration flow within `Auth.tsx`.  The current implementation immediately logs users in after the simulated registration success. I'm exploring enhancements to this initial behavior, specifically focusing on the user experience during registration.


**Refining User Journey**

I'm now implementing the redirect after registration. I've removed the immediate login and introduced a success message. My plan includes a 2-second delay before automatically routing users to the login screen for a smoother experience. Alternatively, I might just switch modes, but I'm leaning toward distinct routes for clarity. Input validation for errors is already in place.


**Adjusting Post-Register Behavior**

I'm now implementing the redirect after registration. Based on the provided code, when registering, the user sees a form (currently the same page), and needs to be redirected to `/login`. Now, I'm considering the best way to handle this: I am considering changing the component based on props, but redirecting to `/login` seems the most correct based on the code's existing flow.


**Enhancing User Feedback**

I'm focusing on providing more visual feedback to the user post-registration. The code now displays a success message, confirming account creation. To improve the user journey, I'm integrating that feedback directly into the UI. I also need to ensure the state is appropriately reset when the form changes modes, either through route changes or prop updates. I'm exploring the nuances of state management within the context of React Router's `<Navigate>` component and direct prop changes to `<Auth mode=`login`>`


**Implementing UI Feedback**

I'm now integrating feedback for registration success. The component will now display a message, confirming the account creation. I've switched to tracking form state internally. I'm focusing on ensuring state resets when the form changes modes, be it through a route change or a prop update, as indicated by the route usage. Specifically, I'm working with the `<Navigate>` component and direct prop changes.


**Managing State Transitions**

I've implemented state management for the registration form using `useState` hooks to manage form fields, errors, and success messages.  Crucially, I'm now incorporating a `useEffect` to clear error and success messages whenever the `mode` prop changes, ensuring a clean slate for the user. I'm also considering clearing the input fields as well for a better UX.


**Optimizing Navigation Logic**

I'm now refining the redirection logic. I'm focusing on ensuring the correct navigation post-login, currently after a registration success. The code navigates to the dashboard upon a successful login. I'm exploring enhancements.


**Improving Feedback Mechanism**

Now, I'm integrating visual feedback after registration success, displaying a message. The component now tracks form state internally. I'm focusing on state reset on mode changes, whether routes or prop changes. I'm using `<Navigate>` and prop updates now to change between the registration and login routes, and ensuring the UI responds correctly.


**Adding Email Validation**

I'm now integrating email validation into the registration form. I'm focusing on ensuring the email field adheres to a standard format before submitting the registration data. I'll need to update the error handling to reflect this.


**Adding Role-Based Fields**

I've integrated a new role selection dropdown into the registration form.  Now, the user can choose their role from a defined list: Club Representative, Advisor, EXCO, or HEPA Staff. This addition will inform future user authorization, based on these roles, and is now integrated as part of registration.


**Refining Redirection Strategy**

I'm now focusing on the redirection logic post-registration. Based on the new form, after a user registers, I'm aiming to display a success message and then automatically redirect them to the login page. I'm exploring the use of `setTimeout` to implement this delay, providing a brief period for the user to see the success message before the redirect occurs.


**Streamlining Redirection Process**

I've implemented the `setTimeout` functionality to redirect users to `/login` after a 2-second delay post-registration. This means I've removed the direct call to `login()` to enhance the flow. I've also incorporated the `success` message into the UI with green styling, similar to the existing error message. I've added a `useEffect` hook to clear both error and success states when the `mode` prop changes, promoting clean state transitions.

To address your request, I will update the `pages/Auth.tsx` file to handle the registration flow correctly. This involves displaying a success message upon successful registration instead of automatically logging the user in, and then redirecting them to the login page after a short delay.

Here are the specific changes:

1.  **Update `pages/Auth.tsx`**:
    *   Add a `success` state to manage the success message.
    *   Update `handleSubmit` to separate registration logic from login logic.
    *   For registration: Set the success message and use `setTimeout` to navigate to `/login` after 2 seconds.
    *   Add a `useEffect` hook to clear form state and messages when switching between 'login' and 'register' modes.
    *   Render the success message in the UI with appropriate styling (green alert box).

entries.description: Update registration flow to show success message and redirect to login instead of auto-login

2) Login Output

Output Design:
Successful login shows login successfull and directs users to their role-based dashboard. If login fails, the system outputs an error message such as “Invalid email or password.”

**Evaluating the Request**

I'm currently breaking down the request to update the login flow. I'm focusing on handling both successful and failed login attempts in `pages/Auth.tsx`. The goal for success is a "Login successful" message followed by redirection to the dashboard. For failures, I'm aiming for an "Invalid email or password" error message.

My aim is to include a brief delay and a success message before navigating to the dashboard, similar to the registration flow. This involves modifying the current code that immediately calls `login()` and `navigate()`. I'll introduce a `setTimeout` to show a temporary success message to the user. I'll also add very basic input validation.

Based on my analysis of the `handleSubmit` function, I am adding input validation, success handling, and a brief delay before navigation. The success case now shows a temporary message. The plan is to call `login()` to update the context after checking email and password, then show the message. I also need to refine the error messages.


**Defining Success & Failure**

My focus has shifted to explicitly defining success and failure scenarios within `handleSubmit` in `pages/Auth.tsx`.  I'm simulating a backend interaction by assuming success if inputs exist.  I'm now implementing the UI error display for the failure case to match the specified output.  The success path updates context and redirects after a success message. I'm leveraging existing UI components for displaying success and error messages.


**Clarifying Authentication Logic**

I'm now fully immersed in the login process within `pages/Auth.tsx`. I've made sure to cover input validation and success and failure cases. After simulating the backend process, I implemented a generic error message for invalid inputs. For successful logins, I call the login function to immediately update context, followed by a short success message and redirection to the dashboard. I've also verified that the redirect in `App.tsx` takes the user to the correct path, ensuring a seamless login experience.


**Implementing Redirection Delay**

I'm now integrating the redirect delay in `handleSubmit` within `pages/Auth.tsx`.  I'm focusing on ensuring users see the "Login successful" message.  I've implemented a `setTimeout` to handle the redirection after the message is displayed.  I've verified that the redirection to the dashboard occurs seamlessly after the delay.


**Consolidating Error Handling**

I'm now consolidating the error handling to ensure clarity and consistency. The success and failure messages are updated to match the specifications perfectly, with "Login successful!" and "Invalid email or password." I'm also ensuring the field validation strictly adheres to the prompt, where empty fields trigger the failure scenario. I am also making sure I integrate the registration logic in the file I will output.


**Refining the Success Logic**

I'm now integrating the `setTimeout` and refining the success message. I've confirmed the previous changes have the registration logic, so I'm focusing on `handleSubmit`'s login success path.  I'm adding the `setTimeout` for the login success message, which will trigger redirection to the dashboard. The success message should be correctly displayed.


**Validating the Navigation**

I'm now carefully examining the navigation timing within the `handleSubmit` function. I have ensured the redirect delay of 1500ms correctly follows the display of the success message and proceeds seamlessly to the dashboard. The integration seems to be working as planned. I also ensured that the redirect goes to the intended dashboard path.

To implement the login feedback requirements, I will update `pages/Auth.tsx`.

**Changes:**
1.  **Update `handleSubmit` (Login Section)**: Instead of immediately redirecting, the system will now:
    *   Validate that fields are not empty (simulating a check). If empty, display "Invalid email or password."
    *   If valid, display a "Login successful!" success message.
    *   Update the auth state.
    *   Wait for 1.5 seconds before redirecting to the Dashboard, allowing the user to see the success message.

entries.description: Update login flow to show success/error messages and delay redirection

4) Proposal Submission Output

Output Design:
Once a proposal is submitted, the system shows a confirmation message and the proposal status becomes “Pending.” The proposal appears in the user’s proposal list and tracking section.

**Updating Submission Flow**

My goal is to add a crucial step: displaying a confirmation message after a successful submission. This will improve the user experience by giving feedback on the submission's status.

Instead of immediate redirection, a success message will appear. I'll need to update `handleSubmit` to set this state after `addProposal` completes.

This approach streamlines the user journey by offering clear feedback post-submission without needing modifications to `DataContext.tsx` as initially thought.

I'm also adding a button to navigate to the user's proposal list. This button replaces the initial immediate redirection, focusing on a smoother, more user-centric approach.

The "View My Proposals" button directs users to their proposal list, replacing the dashboard redirection. The "Return to Dashboard" button features a slate background, slate border, and matching text, offering another clear option for the user. Upon successful submission, the UI will reflect the "Pending" status, mirroring the "clean, modern" design. The `isSubmitted` state will trigger the rendering of the success message and include navigation options.


**Finalizing Success Flow**

I'm focusing on the visual confirmation, including a checkmark icon, the title of the submitted proposal, and its initial pending status. I've also incorporated a "View My Proposals" button to provide users with direct access to their submissions, and I'm ensuring all styling elements align with the design system. I'm focusing on ensuring this message is intuitive, clear, and consistent with the design, building upon the initial feedback loop with a pending status update. I'm aiming for seamless integration of this updated view.

I'll need to carefully ensure that the existing form code and imports are correctly merged. I need to make sure I include all imports and the full form code in the `else` (default return) part. I need to double-check the existing imports to ensure everything is correct and I have all the components needed for both form and success display.


**Improving Status Integration**

I'm now integrating the updated status display within the success message, building on the initial pending advisor status. I'm importing `LayoutDashboard` from `lucide-react` for the dashboard button within the success view. My goal is to streamline the success message and include all necessary UI elements to improve user navigation. I'm focusing on ensuring the new status is accurate and clearly communicates the next steps to the user.


**Enhancing Success Feedback**

**Integrating View Button**

I'm now incorporating the "View My Proposals" button into the success message. This addition aims to streamline the user flow after a successful submission, providing direct access to their list of proposals. This step builds on the existing success confirmation and pending status display, enhancing the feedback loop.


**Implementing Dashboard Button**

The goal is to provide a more controlled navigation, building upon the "View My Proposals" option. I'm focusing on ensuring the button is clearly styled, using the correct icons, and properly connected to the navigation functionality within the `SubmitProposal.tsx` file to further streamline user feedback.


**Adjusting Input Fields**

I'm currently working to streamline the input fields within the form. I want to improve the `Event Title` field by ensuring it's clearly labeled and visually distinct. I'm also ensuring the venue input has an informative placeholder and explanatory note. My goal is to make the form more user-friendly and aesthetically pleasing.

I'm aiming to ensure the labels and placeholders are intuitive and visually appealing. I'm also ensuring the objective text area offers a clear user interface and modern styling.

entries.description: Display confirmation message after proposal submission instead of redirecting immediately

diffs.replacement:
import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'; import { useAuth } from '../contexts/AuthContext'; import { useData } from '../contexts/DataContext'; import { Upload, CheckCircle, FileText, LayoutDashboard } from 'lucide-react'; const SubmitProposal: React.FC = () => { const { user } = useAuth(); const { addProposal } = useData(); const navigate = useNavigate(); const [formData, setFormData] = useState({ title: '', eventDate: '', venue: '', objective: '', participants: '', description: '', }); const [isSubmitted, setIsSubmitted] = useState(false); const handleChange = (e: React.ChangeEvent) => { setFormData({ ...formData, [e.target.name]: e.target.value }); }; const handleSubmit = (e: React.FormEvent) => { e.preventDefault(); if (!user) return; // Simulate Documents const documents = [ { name: 'Paperwork_Draft.pdf', url: '#' }, { name: 'Risk_Assessment.pdf', url: '#' }, ]; addProposal({ ...formData, clubName: user.clubName || 'Unknown Club', submitterName: user.name, dateSubmitted: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0], documents, }); setIsSubmitted(true); }; if (isSubmitted) { return (

Submission Successful!

Your event proposal "{formData.title}" has been submitted.
Current Status: Pending Advisor

); } return (

Submit Event Proposal

Fill in the details to request approval for your club event.

Note: This does not book the venue automatically.

7) Review Decision Output

Output Design:
After the reviewer chooses approve/reject/amend, the system updates the proposal status accordingly and displays a confirmation notice. The decision is recorded in the proposal history log.

**Developing UI Updates**

I'm currently focused on the UI implementation for the "Review Decision Output" feature. My attention is on refining how the proposal review actions (approve, reject, amend) are displayed and managed. I'm prioritizing user experience and streamlining the workflow for reviewers to make decisions effectively.

The focus is on a user-friendly way to submit notes directly within the proposal details page. I'm prioritizing data handling and form submission.

**Adding Comment Styling**

I'm now focusing on the comment styling within the Hepa section. I've been working on visually differentiating the various comment types, such as 'compliance_note,' 'rejection_reason,' and 'amendment_request.' I'm aiming for clear visual cues, including color-coding and icons, to enhance readability.


**Enhancing Comment Display**

I'm currently focused on the styling for the comments section within the proposal details page. I'm prioritizing the clarity of comment display with distinct visual styling for author information and timestamps. I want to improve readability.

**Organizing Timeline Display**

**Styling Post-Event Feedback**

I'm now integrating the visual design for post-event feedback within `ProposalDetails.tsx`. I've used styled components to create a distinctive section. It features a prominent headline "Event Completed?", and a prompt to submit a report. I'm aiming for clarity and a call to action.

To implement the review decision output requirements, I have updated `pages/ProposalDetails.tsx`.

The changes include:
1.  **Success Message State**: Introduced a `successMessage` state variable to manage confirmation notices.
2.  **Confirmation Notice**: Upon confirming a review action (approve, reject, or request amendment), the `handleAction` function now sets a descriptive success message (e.g., "Proposal successfully approved.") which is displayed prominently at the top of the page in a green alert box.
3.  **Auto-Dismiss**: The success message automatically disappears after 4 seconds to keep the UI clean.
4.  **History Recording**: The existing `updateProposalStatus` function in `DataContext` (called by `handleAction`) already handles recording the decision in the history log, ensuring the audit trail is maintained.

entries.description: Add confirmation notice and success message handling for review decisions

8) Post-Event Report Output

Output Design:
Once the report is submitted, the system outputs a successful submission message and updates the event record as completed/archived. The report becomes available for viewing by relevant roles.

**Developing Report Submission**

I'm currently focused on the Post-Event Report submission output. I'm aiming to provide a clear "submission successful" message upon report completion, seamlessly update the associated event record to a "completed" or "archived" state, and ensure the report becomes readily available for viewing.


**Examining Submission Workflow**

I'm now diving into the code within `pages/PostEventReport.tsx`. The current `handleSubmit` function triggers `submitReport`, sets a `success` state, and then redirects after a brief delay. I need to make sure the event record state is changed upon submission. The core logic of triggering that state change seems to be missing.  I'll examine the integration of the report submission to verify that the state change is properly triggered.


**Enhancing Submission Confirmation**

I'm now refining the visual feedback for successful report submissions. The current implementation already marks events as complete, but I'm focusing on making the confirmation screen more impactful. I'll integrate the provided design elements to provide a more visually appealing and informative confirmation to the user.


**Improving Success Display**

I'm now iterating on the success screen, aiming for a cleaner, more modern look and feel. The current display is too simple, so I'm updating it to match the style of other successful interactions, like the `SubmitProposal` flow. I'll include a prominent "view event details" link to let users dive in immediately, rather than just waiting to go back to the dashboard. The goal is a more visually pleasing and immediately useful confirmation.


**Implementing Interactive Success**

I've removed the auto-redirect and replaced it with actionable buttons: "View Event Details" and "Back to Dashboard."  I've implemented a centered card with a green checkmark, a clear success message, and context regarding the event's completion.  The `SubmitProposal` flow served as a solid design template for this enhancement.


**Refining Submission Confirmation**

I'm now revising the feedback loop for successful submissions. I've switched from automatic redirection to an interactive success message with actionable buttons. There's a centered card with a green checkmark, a clear success message, and event status context, drawing from the `SubmitProposal` flow for design inspiration.


**Incorporating Interactive Feedback**

I'm now incorporating actionable buttons into the success display. I've included "View Event Details" and "Back to Dashboard" buttons to provide immediate next steps. The centered card now prominently features a green checkmark and a clear success message. I'm leveraging the design elements of the `SubmitProposal` flow for consistency, improving the user experience and clarifying the context of event completion.


**Adjusting Report Navigation**

I'm now integrating manual navigation options into the success display. I've updated `pages/PostEventReport.tsx` to replace automatic redirection with "View Event Details" and "Back to Dashboard" buttons. The core logic of the form remains unchanged. I'm focusing on user control and clarity after report submission.


**Adding Conditional Rendering**

Currently, I'm integrating conditional rendering based on user roles to restrict access to the report submission functionality. Specifically, I'm ensuring that only club representatives can submit reports. The form now validates that the user is a club representative before displaying the content. This prevents unauthorized access to the report submission. I've added a check that renders a message for users who are not authorized.

**Verifying Role-Based Access**

Now I'm adding role-based access to the report submission page. I'm building checks to verify if the user is a club representative before showing the submission form. A clear message appears for unauthorized users. The goal is to restrict report submission to the appropriate personnel.


**Finalizing Access Controls**

I've just added the final touches to the role-based access restrictions. I've confirmed that only club representatives can see the report submission form; other users receive an informative message. The navigation buttons are also updated. The entire process is now complete.


**Reviewing Empty State Display**

I'm now implementing the display for the scenario with no available reports. The logic already renders a default "no pending reports" message. I will ensure the design is consistent with other empty states in the application and provides a clear user experience.


**Enhancing File Upload Logic**

I'm now integrating the file upload component. It currently supports drag-and-drop or click-to-upload functionality. I need to handle the state of the selected file, rendering the filename, and providing appropriate feedback to the user on success or failure. I'll need to store the file and add checks to handle file type and size.


**Updating File Handling**

I'm now integrating the file upload component, expanding its capabilities to include both document and image uploads. I'm focusing on adding checks for file type, such as PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, and PNG, while also verifying file size limits. I'm aiming to offer users a smooth experience with clear feedback on successful uploads.


**Refining File Upload Validation**

I'm now focusing on file validation within the upload component. I'm adding specific checks for file types (PDF, DOC, DOCX, JPG, PNG) and size restrictions to ensure data integrity and user experience. I'll provide clear feedback on successful uploads or any validation errors encountered.


**Examining Report Display**

I'm now investigating how the uploaded post-event report is displayed. It looks like `ProposalDetails.tsx` currently displays the initial proposal documents but not the `postEventReport` content. I'll need to figure out how to integrate the post-event report details, including the report file and photos, into the existing display.


**Implementing Report Viewing**

I'm now integrating the post-event report display. I'm modifying `pages/ProposalDetails.tsx` to conditionally render the report section if a post-event report exists. I'll include the report file and any associated photos for easy viewing.


**Updating Report Display Logic**

I'm currently working to integrate the post-event report details into the proposal view. I'm focusing on the component logic to ensure the report file and any associated photos display correctly. I'll need to figure out how to conditionally render the report section based on its existence.


**Refining Status Update Actions**

I'm now implementing the update action logic for proposal statuses. It's working to incorporate new statuses like "Approved," "Rejected," and "Needs Improvement." I'm also ensuring a comment is required for "Needs Improvement" and "Rejected" actions and am developing the appropriate confirmation messages to deliver feedback. I'm focusing on the integration of these different logic paths.


**Improving Modal Interactions**

I'm now integrating the modal's interaction with comments.  Currently, the modal handles statuses and comment logic.  I am adding the integration of new compliance notes and hepa tags, ensuring they integrate with the modal's workflow. I'll make sure the comments are also displayed within the modal.


**Adjusting Status Submission**

I'm presently focused on the logic for the proposal status update actions. I've integrated new states like "Approved," "Rejected," and "Needs Improvement." I'm also ensuring comments are obligatory for "Needs Improvement" and "Rejected" actions. The relevant confirmation messages are being developed to provide feedback. I'm prioritizing the smooth integration of the different logic pathways within the component.


**Adjusting Comment Input**

I'm now revising the comment input field for clarity and usability. The comment field is now a resizable textarea with a placeholder. I'm focusing on improved responsiveness in the modal and ensuring the comments are stored correctly. I'll need to make sure the comment field can be clearly identified within the existing modal structure.


**Integrating Enhanced Feedback**

I'm now incorporating design elements into the success message to enhance clarity. I've switched to displaying the submission confirmation as a centered card with a green checkmark, a success message, and context regarding event completion. I'm focusing on providing more visual cues about the submission's status.


**Adding Proposal Review Actions**

I'm presently building the approval workflows. I'm focused on incorporating buttons for approval, rejection, and requesting changes, specifically. These elements will only appear for users with reviewing access.


**Enhancing Edit Access**

I'm presently building the Edit & Resubmit button functionality for proposals. Currently, the button appears. I'm focusing on ensuring it only displays when the proposal is in a "Needs Changes" status. This will improve workflow control and make the page more user-friendly. I'll need to use conditional rendering to show the button.


**Updating Display Details**

I'm now integrating the provided design elements into the proposal display, refining the event details section for better clarity. The components such as event date, venue, participants, objective, and description, now showcase a more organized and user-friendly layout. I'm focusing on ensuring consistent styling and clear information hierarchy within the section.


**Updating Document Display**

I'm now integrating the proposal documents into the detail view. The files are displaying correctly, following design specifications. The display will have an improved look. Now I am working on integrating the post event details. I'll make sure the files are correctly linked.


**Enhancing Document Display**

I'm presently integrating the post-event report document and images into the proposal detail view, ensuring they are properly displayed and styled. The file downloads are being correctly linked. The display should be clearer.


**Improving Image Display**

I'm now integrating the post-event report images into the display. I've added a section to display the report file name and a download button. Additionally, if photos are available, they're now rendered as thumbnails. I'm focusing on ensuring these images display properly.


**Implementing Image Thumbnails**

I'm presently building out the ability to display the uploaded report images as thumbnails.  I'll add the necessary logic to generate thumbnails from the uploaded images within the post event report. These thumbnails will be displayed alongside the report file for easy navigation and viewing.


**Updating Image Thumbnails**

I'm currently working to integrate the display of image thumbnails from the post-event report. I've added the logic needed to create these thumbnails dynamically. Now, users will see small previews of their images alongside the report file for convenient viewing.


**Adding Modal Comment Functionality**

I'm now integrating the provided comment functionality into the modal. The textarea and submit button are added and working. I'm focusing on persisting those inputs.


**Finalizing Comment Persistence**

I'm now integrating the modal's comment saving functionality. The comments will persist correctly to the backend, using the new endpoint `POST /proposals/comments`. I will make sure the comments appear properly on the display.


**Adding Comment Styling**

I'm now styling the comments in the modal. I'm adding styling to make sure that the compliance notes have a distinct appearance. I am using distinct color-coding and icons. I'm making sure that the new compliance note styling is properly integrated.


**Improving Comment Styling**

I'm now styling the comments in the modal. I'm adding styling to make sure the compliance notes have a distinct appearance using color coding and icons. I'm ensuring this new styling integrates well.


**Updating Comment Display**

I'm now focusing on integrating the comment display with the backend data. I've successfully implemented the styling for the compliance notes in the modal. Next, I will be loading and presenting the backend comments within the new stylings to ensure a seamless and informative presentation.


**Enhancing Status Indicator**

I'm presently refactoring the UI to enhance visual clarity. I've switched the UI from using a `progress indicator` to displaying `next-stage indicators` with enhanced visual cues such as status bubbles and labels. I'm focusing on ensuring clear communication.


**Reviewing Report Data Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating the report data display more deeply into the proposal details view. My current progress involves ensuring that the report file name, date, and any uploaded photos are seamlessly integrated within the `ProposalDetails.tsx` component. I'm focusing on creating an accessible and easy-to-read layout for users.

To implement the Post-Event Report Output logic, I will update `pages/PostEventReport.tsx` to display a comprehensive success confirmation screen with navigation options after submission. I will also update `pages/ProposalDetails.tsx` to render the submitted post-event report so it is available for viewing as requested.

entries.description: Enhance post-event report submission success UI with manual navigation options and clearer feedback
Add display for Post-Event Report documents if available
